title: Non-Relativistic Spin-Orbit Interaction in Triplet Superconductors: Edelstein Effect and Spin Pumping by Electric Fields
authors: Ping Li, G. A. Bobkov, I. V. Bobkova, Tao Yu
abstract: Non-relativistic momentum-dependent spin splitting, as observed in collinear altermagnets and non-collinear $p$-wave magnets, provides exciting avenues for controlling spin dynamics. Here, we reveal a distinct form of non-relativistic ``spin-orbit coupling" in triplet superconductors by demonstrating that the triplet order parameter induces a wave-vector-dependent spin texture of Bogoliubov quasiparticles, thereby entangling their orbital and spin motions. Even in the absence of relativistic spin-orbit coupling, this intertwining of spin and orbital motion allows an electric field to generate spin polarization in a $p$-wave superconductor -- that is, an Edelstein effect. Building on this mechanism, we propose an efficient scheme for the nonlinear generation of a DC spin current via electric near fields, driven by AC spin polarization and electron velocity. This general principle offers a powerful route for generating and manipulating spin currents in unconventional superconductors.
summary: This work reveals a unique, non-relativistic spin-orbit coupling mechanism within triplet superconductors. By analyzing the quasiparticle spin texture induced by the superconducting order parameter, the authors show that an applied electric field can generate a spin polarization (Edelstein effect) and a DC spin current. This offers a powerful, field-driven route for manipulating spin currents in unconventional superconductors.
main_problem: The paper investigates a non-relativistic form of spin-orbit coupling in triplet superconductors, enabling spin manipulation using electric fields even without relativistic SOC.
main_result: An in-plane electric field can induce a spin polarization (Edelstein effect) and drive a DC spin current in a p-wave superconductor via intrinsic quasiparticle spin-orbital entanglement.
method: The analysis uses linear response theory applied to the Nambu space Hamiltonian, calculating spin and spin-current densities based on quasiparticle scattering amplitudes.
model_or_system: The system is a triplet p-wave superconducting film, modeled using a Nambu space Hamiltonian. The key physics involves the triplet order parameter inducing momentum-dependent spin textures in Bogoliubov quasiparticles.
key_observables: Spin polarization ($\delta \mathbf{s}$), DC spin-current density ($\mathbf{J}_s$), and spin-torque density ($\mathbf{T}_s$).
important_parameters: The analysis is valid for $\hbar\omega < 2\Delta_t$, and the spin polarization is restricted to the $\hat{z}$-direction for in-plane fields.
main_assumptions: The analysis is performed in the linear response regime, and the model assumes a minimal description of the triplet p-wave superconductor.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the wave-vector-dependent spin of quasiparticles. Figure 2 demonstrates the Spin Edelstein effect, showing $\hat{z}$-polarized spin density induced by an in-plane electric field.
paper_structure: The paper establishes the non-relativistic SOC mechanism via the triplet order parameter, calculates the linear response to external E/H fields, and finally derives the resulting DC spin current and spin polarization.
why_interesting: The mechanism of generating spin currents purely from electric fields in superconductors, without relying on strong relativistic SOC, provides a novel platform for studying spin-charge conversion in unconventional quantum materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Illustration of spin Sz ∈[−ℏ/2, ℏ/2] of Bogoliubov quasiparticles on the two degenerate bands {ε1(k), ε2(k)} of a triplet superconductor in the momentum space. The color maps represent the spin of quasiparticles Sz. Sz depends strongly on the wave vector near the Fermi wave vector kF .

FIG. 2. Spin Edelstein effect in a p-wave superconductor. (a): an in-plane electric field E (red arrow) induces the spin den- sity polarized along the ˆz-direction (blue arrow). (b) & (c): This spin polarization arises from an intra-band quasiparticle scattering process driven by the electric field, as illustrated for band 1 and addressed in the main text.

FIG. 3. Spin pumping into p-wave superconductor driven solely by local electric fields. The shadow region in (a)-(c) indicates the region of the near electric field. (a) illustrates the transverse (blue arrow) and longitudinal (red arrow) spin current pumped by the one-dimensional electromagnetic field. (b) depicts the spatial profile of the transverse spin current induced by the electric field. (c) plots the spatial profile of the spin-torque density polarized along the ˆx- and ˆy-directions excited by the joint effect of electric and magnetic fields.

Spin-momentum entanglement.note: The new paper explicitly analyzes the 'intrinsic quasiparticle spin-orbital entanglement' and the resulting momentum-dependent spin textures, which directly relates to the concept of spin-momentum entanglement.
